title: Hindustan Unilever Limited Reports Strong Q4 and FY26 Performance - Machine Maker
content:
Hindustan Unilever Limited today reported its financial performance for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, delivering a strong close to the fiscal year with broad-based growth across key segments. For the March quarter, the company recorded a turnover of Rs. 16,207 crores, reflecting an 8% year-on-year increase. Underlying sales grew 7%, supported by a 6% rise in volumes, marking the highest quarterly growth in the past 12 quarters.
EBITDA margin improved sequentially to 23.7%, while absolute EBITDA stood at Rs. 3,841 crores, up 6% year-on-year. Profit after tax before exceptional items reached Rs. 2,711 crores, growing 4%, while reported profit after tax rose 20% to Rs. 3,002 crores, aided by gains from a strategic divestment.
The Home Care segment led performance with 9% growth, its strongest showing in nearly three years, driven by robust demand across fabric wash and household care. The liquids portfolio maintained strong momentum, complemented by improved performance in powders and bars.
Beauty & Wellbeing posted 8% growth, supported by strong double-digit expansion in hair care. Premium skin care and cosmetics continued to perform well, while new product launches and focused channel strategies contributed to market share gains.
Personal Care grew 5%, with skin cleansing delivering high-single digit growth led by brands such as Dove and Lux. Innovation remained a focus, including expansion into premium and freshness-driven segments.
The Foods business also reported 5% growth, with coffee and lifestyle nutrition emerging as key drivers. Products such as Horlicks and Boost performed strongly, while new product introductions and premiumisation initiatives supported category expansion.
For the full financial year, turnover stood at Rs. 63,763 crores, up 5%, with EBITDA margin at 23.6%. Profit after tax before exceptional items reached Rs. 10,324 crores, while reported profit after tax was Rs. 10,652 crores. The Board has proposed a final dividend of Rs. 22 per share, taking the total annual payout to Rs. 9,633 crores.
Commenting on the performance, Priya Nair highlighted improved demand conditions and strategic actions taken during the year to accelerate growth, strengthen capabilities, and enhance execution. She noted that while global uncertainties persist, the company remains well positioned to sustain competitive growth through strong brands, operational resilience, and disciplined cost management.
Hindustan Unilever Limited (HUL) is one of India’s largest and most prominent fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies. A subsidiary of the global consumer goods giant Unilever, HUL operates in diverse sectors, including food and beverages, home and personal care, and health and wellness products.
Established in 1933, the company has built a strong portfolio of well-known brands, such as Dove, Lipton, Surf Excel, and Knorr, which have become household names in India and beyond. HUL is renowned for its commitment to sustainability, with initiatives focused on reducing its environmental footprint, promoting responsible sourcing, and supporting community development. As a market leader, HUL continues to adapt to evolving consumer needs, innovating with new products and solutions while maintaining a strong emphasis on quality, trust, and social responsibility.
